channel.title: Brisbane s calendar Light up Brisbane
channel.description: This calendar includes scheduled lights ups of Council assets including Story Bridge, Reddacliff Place sculptures, Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City Hall, King George Square, William Jolly Bridge and Tropical Dome at Brisbane Botanic Gardens Mt Coot-tha.

item.title: Spark the Night for Parkinson's Awareness
item.description:
Friday, 10 April 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Blue to support Spark the Night for Parkinson's Awareness

#SparktheNight helps people and organisations come together to shine a light on Parkinson's, fostering hope, solidarity, and an increased understanding of the challenges for the 12 million people living with Parkinson’s worldwide.
Parkinson’s Australia is the national voice and advocate for people living with Parkinson’s, their families and carers, researchers, and health professionals.
We are committed to connecting our community through nationwide engagement, and we welcome open communication. We work to reduce the impact of Parkinson’s by promoting awareness and best practice care, ensuring that people can maximise their opportunities to live well and maintain their independence.


This Light Up is organised by: Parkinson's Australia

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:summary: Spark the Night for Parkinson's Awareness
item.xCal:description:
Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Blue to support Spark the Night for Parkinson's Awareness
#SparktheNight helps people and organisations come together to shine a light on Parkinson's, fostering hope, solidarity, and an increased understanding of the challenges for the 12 million people living with Parkinson’s worldwide. Parkinson’s Australia is the national voice and advocate for people living with Parkinson’s, their families and carers, researchers, and health professionals. We are committed to connecting our community through nationwide engagement, and we welcome open communication. We work to reduce the impact of Parkinson’s by promoting awareness and best practice care, ensuring that people can maximise their opportunities to live well and maintain their independence.
This Light Up is organised by: Parkinson's Australia
item.x-trumba:customfield: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: Spark the Night for Parkinson's Awareness
item.description:
Friday, 10 April 2026

City Hall will be lit Blue to support Spark the Night for Parkinson's Awareness

#SparktheNight helps people and organisations come together to shine a light on Parkinson's, fostering hope, solidarity, and an increased understanding of the challenges for the 12 million people living with Parkinson’s worldwide.
Parkinson’s Australia is the national voice and advocate for people living with Parkinson’s, their families and carers, researchers, and health professionals.
We are committed to connecting our community through nationwide engagement, and we welcome open communication. We work to reduce the impact of Parkinson’s by promoting awareness and best practice care, ensuring that people can maximise their opportunities to live well and maintain their independence.


This Light Up is organised by: Parkinson's Australia

Light up asset: Brisbane City Hall, Brisbane City
Venue address: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Library, Light up asset
item.xCal:summary: Spark the Night for Parkinson's Awareness
item.xCal:description:
City Hall will be lit Blue to support Spark the Night for Parkinson's Awareness
#SparktheNight helps people and organisations come together to shine a light on Parkinson's, fostering hope, solidarity, and an increased understanding of the challenges for the 12 million people living with Parkinson’s worldwide. Parkinson’s Australia is the national voice and advocate for people living with Parkinson’s, their families and carers, researchers, and health professionals. We are committed to connecting our community through nationwide engagement, and we welcome open communication. We work to reduce the impact of Parkinson’s by promoting awareness and best practice care, ensuring that people can maximise their opportunities to live well and maintain their independence.

Parkinson’s Australia is the national voice and advocate for people living with Parkinson’s, their families and carers, researchers, and health professionals.

#SparktheNight helps people and organisations come together to shine a light on Parkinson's, fostering hope, solidarity, and an increased understanding of the challenges for the 12 million people living with Parkinson’s worldwide.
Parkinson’s Australia is the national voice and advocate for people living with Parkinson’s, their families and carers, researchers, and health professionals.

#SparktheNight helps people and organisations come together to shine a light on Parkinson's, fostering hope, solidarity, and an increased understanding of the challenges for the 12 million people living with Parkinson’s worldwide.
Parkinson’s Australia is the national voice and advocate for people living with Parkinson’s, their families and carers, researchers, and health professionals.
We are committed to connecting our community through nationwide engagement, and we welcome open communication. We work to reduce the impact of Parkinson’s by promoting awareness and best practice care, ensuring that people can maximise their opportunities to live well and maintain their independence.

item.xCal:description:

#SparktheNight helps people and organisations come together to shine a light on Parkinson's, fostering hope, solidarity, and an increased understanding of the challenges for the 12 million people living with Parkinson’s worldwide. Parkinson’s Australia is the national voice and advocate for people living with Parkinson’s, their families and carers, researchers, and health professionals. We are committed to connecting our community through nationwide engagement, and we welcome open communication. We work to reduce the impact of Parkinson’s by promoting awareness and best practice care, ensuring that people can maximise their opportunities to live well and maintain their independence.
This Light Up is organised by: Parkinson's Australia

item.title: Tour De Brisbane
item.description:
Saturday, 11 April 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Pink to celebrate Tour De Brisbane

As a legacy of the Commonwealth Games, the Tour de Brisbane has become an event unique on the World Stage - no other mass bike ride and race is based in the heart of a City bringing together all participants in one unified start and finish location. The event offers a ride for everyone with racing, riding, and family-friendly distances from 120km to 4km all starting from Queens Wharf and finishing into the Cultural Forecourt. The event raises funds for The Common Good, an initiative of The Prince Charles Hospital Foundation.

This Light Up is organised by: NX Sports Community Foundation Limited

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Pink to celebrate Tour De Brisbane
As a legacy of the Commonwealth Games, the Tour de Brisbane has become an event unique on the World Stage - no other mass bike ride and race is based in the heart of a City bringing together all participants in one unified start and finish location. The event offers a ride for everyone with racing, riding, and family-friendly distances from 120km to 4km all starting from Queens Wharf and finishing into the Cultural Forecourt. The event raises funds for The Common Good, an initiative of The Prince Charles Hospital Foundation.
This Light Up is organised by: NX Sports Community Foundation Limited
item.x-trumba:customfield: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: 50 Years of Queensland SES
item.description:
Sunday, 12 April 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Orange to celebrate 50 Years of Queensland SES

The SES has reached its 50 year milestone. Throughout the month of April, Brisbane Region SES will be commemorating the SES spirit and history, honouring our volunteers - past and present and celebrating the partnerships that make the SES what it is today.

This Light Up is organised by: Queensland State Emergency Services

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Orange to celebrate 50 Years of Queensland SES
The SES has reached its 50 year milestone. Throughout the month of April, Brisbane Region SES will be commemorating the SES spirit and history, honouring our volunteers - past and present and celebrating the partnerships that make the SES what it is today.
This Light Up is organised by: Queensland State Emergency Services
item.x-trumba:customfield: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: Youth Homelessness Matters Day
item.description:
Wednesday, 15 April 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Orange to support Youth Homelessness Matters Day

Youth Homelessness Matters Day (YHMD) is a national awareness and advocacy day held each year on the third Wednesday of April. Established in 1990, the day aims to raise awareness of child and youth homelessness in Australia, challenge misconceptions, and encourage community and government action to create lasting solutions. YHMD also celebrates the strength and resilience of young people while calling for safe, appropriate housing and support to prevent youth homelessness. The campaign is coordinated by YFoundations, the peak body for youth homelessness in New South Wales, and supported by other organisations around the country, including Property Industry Foundation.

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Youth Service

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Orange to support Youth Homelessness Matters Day
Youth Homelessness Matters Day (YHMD) is a national awareness and advocacy day held each year on the third Wednesday of April. Established in 1990, the day aims to raise awareness of child and youth homelessness in Australia, challenge misconceptions, and encourage community and government action to create lasting solutions. YHMD also celebrates the strength and resilience of young people while calling for safe, appropriate housing and support to prevent youth homelessness. The campaign is coordinated by YFoundations, the peak body for youth homelessness in New South Wales, and supported by other organisations around the country, including Property Industry Foundation.
This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Youth Service
item.x-trumba:customfield: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: World Voice Day
item.description:
Thursday, 16 April 2026

Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Red to support World Voice Day

World Voice Day is traditionally celebrated on 16 April and allows us to 'shine a light' on voice disorders and raise awareness of voice health issues.
World Voice Day was founded in Brazil back in 1999 with the aim of drawing attention to the science and phenomenon of voice production. It soon grew to highlight the significant role that the voice plays in every aspect of our daily lives. World Voice Day has gained momentum across the globe, and currently over 50 countries join the annual celebration during the month of April.

This Light Up is organised by: Australian Dysphonia Network Incorporated

Light up asset: Reddacliff Place, Brisbane City
Venue address: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Red to support World Voice Day
World Voice Day is traditionally celebrated on 16 April and allows us to 'shine a light' on voice disorders and raise awareness of voice health issues. World Voice Day was founded in Brazil back in 1999 with the aim of drawing attention to the science and phenomenon of voice production. It soon grew to highlight the significant role that the voice plays in every aspect of our daily lives. World Voice Day has gained momentum across the globe, and currently over 50 countries join the annual celebration during the month of April.
This Light Up is organised by: Australian Dysphonia Network Incorporated
item.x-trumba:customfield: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City

item.title: World Voice Day
item.description:
Thursday, 16 April 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Red to support World Voice Day

World Voice Day is traditionally celebrated on 16 April and allows us to 'shine a light' on voice disorders and raise awareness of voice health issues.
World Voice Day was founded in Brazil back in 1999 with the aim of drawing attention to the science and phenomenon of voice production. It soon grew to highlight the significant role that the voice plays in every aspect of our daily lives. World Voice Day has gained momentum across the globe, and currently over 50 countries join the annual celebration during the month of April.

This Light Up is organised by: Australian Dysphonia Network Incorporated

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Victoria Bridge will be lit Red to support World Voice Day
World Voice Day is traditionally celebrated on 16 April and allows us to 'shine a light' on voice disorders and raise awareness of voice health issues. World Voice Day was founded in Brazil back in 1999 with the aim of drawing attention to the science and phenomenon of voice production. It soon grew to highlight the significant role that the voice plays in every aspect of our daily lives. World Voice Day has gained momentum across the globe, and currently over 50 countries join the annual celebration during the month of April.
This Light Up is organised by: Australian Dysphonia Network Incorporated

item.title: World Haemophilia Day
item.description:
Friday, 17 April 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Red to support World Haemophilia Day

World Haemophilia Day raises awareness of haemophilia, von Willebrand disease and other inherited bleeding disorders. This is a critical effort since with increased awareness comes better diagnosis and access to care for the millions who remain without treatment. The Haemophilia Foundation Queensland is a non-for-profit organisation which provides representation, health promotion, education, and support for people in Queensland affected by inherited bleeding disorders

This Light Up is organised by: Haemophilia Foundation Queensland

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Victoria Bridge will be lit Red to support World Haemophilia Day
World Haemophilia Day raises awareness of haemophilia, von Willebrand disease and other inherited bleeding disorders. This is a critical effort since with increased awareness comes better diagnosis and access to care for the millions who remain without treatment. The Haemophilia Foundation Queensland is a non-for-profit organisation which provides representation, health promotion, education, and support for people in Queensland affected by inherited bleeding disorders
This Light Up is organised by: Haemophilia Foundation Queensland

item.title: Citizenship Ceremony
item.description:
Monday, 20 April 2026

City Hall will be lit Green and Gold to celebrate a Citizenship Ceremony

Citizenship ceremonies are an important opportunity to welcome new citizens formally as full members of the Australian community

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council

Light up asset: Brisbane City Hall, Brisbane City
Venue address: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Library, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
City Hall will be lit Green and Gold to celebrate a Citizenship Ceremony
Citizenship ceremonies are an important opportunity to welcome new citizens formally as full members of the Australian community
This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane City Council
item.x-trumba:customfield: Brisbane City Hall, 64 Adelaide Street, Brisbane City

item.title: Brisbane Comedy Festival
item.description:
Thursday, 23 April 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Red, Blue and Yellow to celebrate the Brisbane Comedy Festival

 The home of laughs. Brisbane Comedy Festival returns from · 24 April – 24 May 2026 and is set to deliver the best LOL’s over 5 weeks at 4 venues with over 130 artists!

This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Powerhouse

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Red, Blue and Yellow to celebrate the Brisbane Comedy Festival
The home of laughs. Brisbane Comedy Festival returns from · 24 April – 24 May 2026 and is set to deliver the best LOL’s over 5 weeks at 4 venues with over 130 artists!
This Light Up is organised by: Brisbane Powerhouse
item.x-trumba:customfield: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: Eve of ANZAC Day
item.description:
Friday, 24 April 2026

Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Green and Gold to commemorate the Eve of ANZAC Day

As we prepare to remember the legacy of ANZAC, tonight we honour all members of the ADF who have returned from Overseas service and remember those who did not. The Anzac Day commemorations are an opportunity for the citizens of Queensland to recognise the sacrifice of the ADF and Allies in the defence of Australia.

This Light Up is organised by: Returned Services League of Australia (Qld Branch) South Eastern District Limited

Light up asset: Reddacliff Place, Brisbane City
Venue address: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Green and Gold to commemorate the Eve of ANZAC Day
As we prepare to remember the legacy of ANZAC, tonight we honour all members of the ADF who have returned from Overseas service and remember those who did not. The Anzac Day commemorations are an opportunity for the citizens of Queensland to recognise the sacrifice of the ADF and Allies in the defence of Australia.
This Light Up is organised by: Returned Services League of Australia (Qld Branch) South Eastern District Limited
item.x-trumba:customfield: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City

item.description:
Friday, 24 April 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Green and Gold, Turning to Red at 3am to commemorate the Eve of ANZAC Day

item.title: ANZAC Day
item.description:
Saturday, 25 April 2026

Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Red to commemorate ANZAC Day

As the sun rose on 25 April 1915, Australian and New Zealand soldiers rowed towards the shores of Gallipoli and into history. Every year since, Australians have gathered at Dawn Services or services of Remembrance, at parades, or in their driveways each year to remember their legacy and that of all who have followed in their footsteps.
The first ANZAC Day Parade took place in Brisbane on the 25 April 1916. While we remember those who paid the ultimate sacrifice for freedom during the Dawn Services and other services of Remembrance, the ANZAC Day Parade is an opportunity to celebrate and recognise all those that have served and still continue to serve our country.
The parade will be held on Adelaide Street, between George Street and Creek Street. From the Governor of Queensland’s address at King George Square at 9:45am to the last marching participants at 12:25pm, it will feature former and current ADF veterans and other support organisations that contribute to the Parade that make it the commemoration worthy of its heritage.

This Light Up is organised by: Returned Services League of Australia (Qld Branch) South Eastern District Limited

Light up asset: Reddacliff Place, Brisbane City
Venue address: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Reddacliff Place sculptures will be lit Red to commemorate ANZAC Day
As the sun rose on 25 April 1915, Australian and New Zealand soldiers rowed towards the shores of Gallipoli and into history. Every year since, Australians have gathered at Dawn Services or services of Remembrance, at parades, or in their driveways each year to remember their legacy and that of all who have followed in their footsteps. The first ANZAC Day Parade took place in Brisbane on the 25 April 1916. While we remember those who paid the ultimate sacrifice for freedom during the Dawn Services and other services of Remembrance, the ANZAC Day Parade is an opportunity to celebrate and recognise all those that have served and still continue to serve our country. The parade will be held on Adelaide Street, between George Street and Creek Street. From the Governor of Queensland’s address at King George Square at 9:45am to the last marching participants at 12:25pm, it will feature former and current ADF veterans and other support organisations that contribute to the Parade that make it the commemoration worthy of its heritage.
This Light Up is organised by: Returned Services League of Australia (Qld Branch) South Eastern District Limited
item.x-trumba:customfield: Reddacliff Place, 266 George Street, Brisbane City

The first ANZAC Day Parade took place in Brisbane on the 25 April 1916. While we remember those who paid the ultimate sacrifice for freedom during the Dawn Services and other services of Remembrance, the ANZAC Day Parade is an opportunity to celebrate and recognise all those that have served and still continue to serve our country.
The parade will be held on Adelaide Street, between George Street and Creek Street. item.title: International Dance Day
item.description:
Monday, 27 April 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Red to celebrate International Dance Day

On International Dance Day, we aim for Brisbane City and QLD to recognise and celebrate everyone involved in the Queensland dance sector - students, teachers, professional dancers, choreographers, volunteers, Cultural dance groups, community dance groups, and dance studios. The day is an opportunity for the whole dance community to come together and celebrate all things dance!

This Light Up is organised by: Ausdance QLD

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset
item.xCal:description:
Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Red to celebrate International Dance Day
On International Dance Day, we aim for Brisbane City and QLD to recognise and celebrate everyone involved in the Queensland dance sector - students, teachers, professional dancers, choreographers, volunteers, Cultural dance groups, community dance groups, and dance studios. The day is an opportunity for the whole dance community to come together and celebrate all things dance!
This Light Up is organised by: Ausdance QLD
item.x-trumba:customfield: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point

item.title: Eve of International Guide Dog Day
item.description:
Tuesday, 28 April 2026

Victoria Bridge will be lit Orange to support the Eve of International Guide Dog Day

International Guide Dog Day is an opportunity to bring awareness and advocate for those living with low vision and blindness in our community. At Guide Dogs, we empower people with low vision or blindness to achieve independence, participation, inclusion and wellbeing

This Light Up is organised by: Guide Dogs Queensland

Light up asset: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue address: Victoria Bridge, Brisbane City
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

This Light Up is organised by: Guide Dogs Queensland

item.title: World Wish Day
item.description:
Wednesday, 29 April 2026

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Blue to support World Wish Day

World Wish Day (April 29th) is an annual event that marks the very first wish grated by Make-A-Wish in 1980. It's a time to celebrate the power of a wish and all of the incredible people who come together to help make them come true..

This Light Up is organised by: Make-A-Wish Foundation of Australia Ltd

Light up asset: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Kangaroo Point
Venue address: Kangaroo Point Bridge, Scott Street, Kangaroo Point
Venue type: General Council venue, Light up asset

Kangaroo Point Bridge will be lit Blue to support World Wish Day

This Light Up is organised by: Make-A-Wish Foundation of Australia Ltd
